title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Health Indicators for New Zealanders with Intellectual Disability Publication date: 2 September 2011 This report presents a selection of health status and health care utilisation indicators for New Zealanders with and without intellectual disability. Publication Disability Intellectual disabilities Statistics

## Health Indicators for New Zealanders with Intellectual Disability
This report presents a selection of health status and health care utilisation indicators for New Zealanders with and without intellectual disability.
- Intellectual disabilities
- National Health Emergency Plan: Mass casualty action plan Publication date: 2 September 2011 The National Health Emergency Plan: Mass Casualty Action Plan provides strategic direction and co-ordination to the health sector in the event of an incident resulting in a large number of casualties. Publication Natural disasters Emergency departments

## National Health Emergency Plan: Mass casualty action plan
The National Health Emergency Plan: Mass Casualty Action Plan provides strategic direction and co-ordination to the health sector in the event of an incident resulting in a large number of casualties.
- Emergency departments
- National Health Emergency Plan: Multiple Complex Burn Action Plan Publication date: 2 September 2011 The National Health Emergency Plan: Multiple Complex Burn Action Plan provides direction to the health sector in the event of a burn emergency in New Zealand. Publication Emergency management Hospitals & specialist care

## National Health Emergency Plan: Multiple Complex Burn Action Plan
The National Health Emergency Plan: Multiple Complex Burn Action Plan provides direction to the health sector in the event of a burn emergency in New Zealand.
- Emergency management
- Hospitals & specialist care
- A Focus on Nutrition: Key findings from the 2008/09 NZ Adult Nutrition Survey Publication date: 2 September 2011 This report presents key findings from the 2008/09 New Zealand Adult Nutrition Survey, which was carried out from October 2008 to October 2009. Publication Adults (19–64 years) Nutrition Older people (65+) Statistics Surveys

## A Focus on Nutrition: Key findings from the 2008/09 NZ Adult Nutrition Survey
This report presents key findings from the 2008/09 New Zealand Adult Nutrition Survey, which was carried out from October 2008 to October 2009.
- Adults (19–64 years)
- Whānau Ora : Transforming our futures Publication date: 2 August 2011 Whānau Ora is an approach that empowers New Zealand whānau to transform their futures by taking control of their lives. Introduced in 2010, Whānau Ora is driven by a focus on outcomes: that whānau will be self-managing, living healthy lifestyles, participating fully in society, economically secure, successfully involved in wealth creation, cohesive, resilient and nurturing. Publication Māori health

## Whānau Ora : Transforming our futures
Whānau Ora is an approach that empowers New Zealand whānau to transform their futures by taking control of their lives. Introduced in 2010, Whānau Ora is driven by a focus on outcomes: that whānau will be self-managing, living healthy lifestyles, participating fully in society, economically secure, successfully involved in wealth creation, cohesive, resilient and nurturing.
- New Zealand Maternity Standards Publication date: 2 July 2011 The New Zealand Maternity Standards is a guide for the provision of equitable, safe and high-quality maternity services throughout New Zealand. The Standards are part of the Maternity Quality Initiative. Publication Maternity

## New Zealand Maternity Standards
The New Zealand Maternity Standards is a guide for the provision of equitable, safe and high-quality maternity services throughout New Zealand. The Standards are part of the Maternity Quality Initiative.
- Medicines Care Guides For Residential Aged Care Publication date: 2 June 2011 The aim of the Medicines Care Guides is to provide a quick medicine management reference tool for all care staff working in residential aged care in New Zealand. Publication Older people (65+) Medicine issues Certification of health care services

## Medicines Care Guides For Residential Aged Care
The aim of the Medicines Care Guides is to provide a quick medicine management reference tool for all care staff working in residential aged care in New Zealand.
- Certification of health care services
- Alcohol factsheets Publication date: 9 December 2010 These alcohol factsheets outline New Zealand’s drinking patterns and alcohol-related health, crime and violence, and drink driving. Publication Alcohol
These alcohol factsheets outline New Zealand’s drinking patterns and alcohol-related health, crime and violence, and drink driving.
- Our Oral Health: Key findings of the 2009 New Zealand Oral Health Survey Publication date: 2 December 2010 This report presents key clinical and self-reported findings from the 2009 New Zealand Oral Health Survey, focusing on oral health status, protective factors and service utilisation among the New Zealand population. Publication Oral health Statistics Surveys

## Our Oral Health: Key findings of the 2009 New Zealand Oral Health Survey
This report presents key clinical and self-reported findings from the 2009 New Zealand Oral Health Survey, focusing on oral health status, protective factors and service utilisation among the New Zealand population.
- Interventions and Treatment for Problematic Use of Methamphetamine and other Amphetamine-Type Stimulants (ATS) Publication date: 2 November 2010 These guidelines have been developed for those working with people adversely affected by methamphetamine use. It is a useful resource for frontline workers, including those in mental health services, the police, Department of Corrections, emergency departments, general practitioners, primary health care workers and the specialist addiction treatment sector. Publication Drugs Mental health

## Interventions and Treatment for Problematic Use of Methamphetamine and other Amphetamine-Type Stimulants (ATS)
These guidelines have been developed for those working with people adversely affected by methamphetamine use. It is a useful resource for frontline workers, including those in mental health services, the police, Department of Corrections, emergency departments, general practitioners, primary health care workers and the specialist addiction treatment sector.
- A National Survey of Children and Young People's Physical Activity and Dietary Behaviours in New Zealand: 2008/09: Key Findings Publication date: 6 September 2010 This report presents key findings of the national survey of physical activity, sedentary behaviours and dietary habits in five to 24 year-olds in New Zealand. The survey was commissioned by SPARC together with the Ministries of Health, Education and Youth Development. Publication Child health Youth health Nutrition Physical activity Surveys

## A National Survey of Children and Young People's Physical Activity and Dietary Behaviours in New Zealand: 2008/09: Key Findings
This report presents key findings of the national survey of physical activity, sedentary behaviours and dietary habits in five to 24 year-olds in New Zealand. The survey was commissioned by SPARC together with the Ministries of Health, Education and Youth Development.
- Credentialling Framework for New Zealand Health Professionals Publication date: 2 July 2010 This document explains credentialling for all health professionals, the definition of credentialling, and the credentialling process. Publication Workforce

## Credentialling Framework for New Zealand Health Professionals
This document explains credentialling for all health professionals, the definition of credentialling, and the credentialling process.
- Te Ariari o te Oranga : The Assessment and Management of People with Co-existing Mental Health and Drug Problems Publication date: 2 April 2010 The publication presents a clinical framework to assist health professionals working with co-existing substance use and mental health problems (CEP). It is designed as a companion document to the Ministry of Health's guidance, Service Delivery for People with Co-existing Mental Health and Addiction Problems - Integrated Solutions 2010. Publication Drugs Mental health

## Te Ariari o te Oranga : The Assessment and Management of People with Co-existing Mental Health and Drug Problems
The publication presents a clinical framework to assist health professionals working with co-existing substance use and mental health problems (CEP). It is designed as a companion document to the Ministry of Health's guidance, Service Delivery for People with Co-existing Mental Health and Addiction Problems - Integrated Solutions 2010.
- Customary Tattooing Guidelines for Operators Publication date: 2 April 2010 These guidelines address measures to improve the safety of customary tattooing and are of relevance to tattooists, public health units and Medical Officers of Health. Publication Pacific health Public health

## Customary Tattooing Guidelines for Operators
These guidelines address measures to improve the safety of customary tattooing and are of relevance to tattooists, public health units and Medical Officers of Health.
- Kōrero Mārama : Health Literacy and Māori Results from the 2006 Adult Literacy and Life Skills Survey Publication date: 2 February 2010 The report describes the health literacy skills of Māori compared to non-Māori, based on data from the 2006 Adult Literacy and Lifeskills Survey. Publication Health literacy Adults (19–64 years) Māori health

## Kōrero Mārama : Health Literacy and Māori Results from the 2006 Adult Literacy and Life Skills Survey
The report describes the health literacy skills of Māori compared to non-Māori, based on data from the 2006 Adult Literacy and Lifeskills Survey.
